2605.22650.title: Whose Voice Counts? Mapping Stakeholder Perspectives on AI Through Public Submissions to the U.S. Government
2605.22650.paper_summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems become more common in our daily lives, it is important to understand how different stakeholders comprehend and envisage the role that these technologies play in shaping social, political, and economic realities. In this paper, we investigate public perceptions of AI based on a corpus of letters submitted during the public consultation for the Trump Administration's US AI Action Plan. To this aim, we release a corpus cleaning pipeline and perform topic modelling and frequency analysis to explore predominant topics discussed by different subgroups (e.g., academia, individuals, private sector) and those appearing in the AI Action Plan. Our results show that individuals voice strong concerns related to the impact of AI on life, while other stakeholders are more concerned with AI development. Our comparison of topics suggests that the AI Action Plan reflects predominantly the concerns of the private sector on security, policies, and development, with individuals' concerns less represented.
2605.22650.paper_summary_zh: 隨著人工智慧（AI）系統在我們日常生活中變得越來越普遍，了解不同利益相關者如何理解和想像這些技術在塑造社會、政治和經濟現實中的角色變得至關重要。<lb/>在本文中，我們基於特朗普政府的美國AI行動計畫公共諮詢期間提交的信件語料庫，調查公眾對AI的看法。<lb/>為此，我們發布了一個語料庫清理管道，並進行主題建模和頻率分析，以探索不同子群體（例如學術界、個人、私營部門）和出現在AI行動計畫中的主要討論主題。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，個人對AI對生活的影響表達了強烈的擔憂，而其他利益相關者則更關心AI的發展。<lb/>我們對主題的比較表明，AI行動計畫主要反映了私營部門在安全、政策和發展方面的擔憂，而個人的擔憂則較少被代表。
2605.22650.author: Alina Karakanta et.al.
2605.22650.authors: Alina Karakanta, Alex Christiansen, Tomás Dodds, Bissie Anderson, Matteo Fuoli, Marcus Perlman, Aletta G. Dorst
